title: AI Harness Engineering: A Runtime Substrate for Foundation-Model Software Agents
abstract: Foundation models have transformed automated code generation, yet autonomous software-engineering agents remain unreliable in realistic development settings. The dominant explanation locates this gap in model capability. We propose a different locus: software-engineering capability emerges from a model-harness-environment system, in which a runtime substrate -- the harness -- mediates how a foundation-model agent observes a project, acts on it, receives feedback, and establishes that a change is complete. We formalize this substrate as an AI Harness Engineering and identify eleven component responsibilities: task specification, context selection, tool access, project memory, task state, observability, failure attribution, verification, permissions, entropy auditing, and intervention recording. We operationalize the harness through a four-level ladder (H0-H3) that progressively exposes runtime support to the agent, and we propose a trace-based evaluation protocol that converts each agent run into an auditable episode package. Applied to a controlled validation task, the framework yields episode packages whose evidence structure varies systematically with harness level: lower levels produce only a final patch, higher levels produce reproduction logs, failure attributions, deterministic requirement checks, and structured verification reports. The framework reframes the central question of autonomous software engineering from whether a foundation model can produce a patch to whether the model-harness-environment system can produce a verifiably correct, attributed, and maintainable change. We outline a research program for the runtime systems that foundation-model software agents will require.

relevance.en: The paper's trace-based evaluation protocol directly extends your interest in realistic benchmarks beyond pass@k by providing a framework for auditable, verifiable agent runs that capture execution and integration. Its failure attribution component is tangential to your interest in execution feedback, but the paper focuses on system infrastructure rather than post-training methods.
relevance.zh: 本文的基于轨迹的评估协议直接扩展了您对超越pass@k的现实基准的兴趣，它提供了一个框架，用于可审计、可验证的代理运行，捕获执行和集成。其失败归因组件与您对执行反馈的兴趣有间接关联，但本文侧重于系统基础设施而非训练后方法。

caption.zh: 模型-支架-环境系统。基础模型提供潜在的推理和编码能力。软件环境提供仓库、测试、工具、日志和构建能力。AI支架工程位于它们之间，中介上下文、行动、反馈和验证证据。自主软件工程能力是组合系统的属性，而非模型单独所有。

figure.reference_text: by runtime structure. When that structure is present and well-designed, the system performs as if the model is competent. When it is absent or unstable, the same model appears incompetent. Figure 1

brief_summary.en:
Autonomous software-engineering agents powered by foundation models remain unreliable in realistic development settings, despite advances in code generation. Prior work blames this on model capability gaps, but this paper argues the real bottleneck is the missing runtime infrastructure—the "harness"—that mediates how an agent observes a project, takes actions, receives feedback, and verifies completion. Without a systematic substrate, agent runs are brittle, unobservable, and unverifiable, making them unsuitable for production use.

The paper formalizes AI Harness Engineering, identifying eleven component responsibilities (e.g., task specification, failure attribution, verification) and a four-level ladder (H0–H3) that progressively exposes runtime support. In a controlled validation task, higher harness levels produced auditable episode packages with reproduction logs, failure attributions, and verification reports, while lower levels yielded only a final patch. This reframes the central question from "can the model produce a patch?" to "can the model–harness–environment system produce a verifiably correct, attributed, and maintainable change?" The trace-based evaluation protocol offers a more realistic benchmark than pass@k by capturing execution, integration, and maintainability within a project context.
brief_summary.zh:
尽管代码生成技术取得了进展，但基于基础模型的自主软件工程代理在实际开发环境中仍然不可靠。先前的研究将此归咎于模型能力差距，但本文认为真正的瓶颈在于缺失的运行时基础设施——即“支架”（harness）——它中介了代理如何观察项目、采取行动、接收反馈以及验证完成。没有系统化的基础，代理的运行是脆弱的、不可观察且不可验证的，使其不适合生产使用。

本文形式化了AI支架工程（AI Harness Engineering），识别了十一个组件职责（例如，任务规范、失败归因、验证）和一个四级阶梯（H0–H3），该阶梯逐步暴露运行时支持。在一个受控的验证任务中，更高的支架级别产生了可审计的回合包，包含复现日志、失败归因和验证报告，而较低的级别仅产生一个最终的补丁。这将核心问题从“模型能否生成补丁？”重新定义为“模型-支架-环境系统能否产生一个可验证正确、可归因且可维护的变更？”基于轨迹的评估协议通过捕获项目上下文中的执行、集成和可维护性，提供了一个比pass@k更现实的基准。

problem.en: The core problem is that autonomous software-engineering agents powered by foundation models remain unreliable in realistic development settings, despite significant advances in automated code generation. The dominant explanation in prior work attributes this unreliability to a gap in model capability—i.e., the model is not smart enough or lacks sufficient coding knowledge. The paper argues that this explanation is incomplete and misdirected. The concrete gap is that prior work has almost entirely focused on improving the model itself (e.g., larger models, better training data, fine-tuning) while neglecting the runtime infrastructure that mediates how the model interacts with the software environment. In practice, this matters because even a highly capable model will fail if it cannot properly observe a project's state, receive meaningful feedback from failed builds or tests, attribute failures to specific changes, or verify that a fix is complete. The missing piece is a systematic, formalized runtime substrate—what the paper calls a 'harness'—that structures the agent's perception, action, feedback, and verification loops. Without such a substrate, agent runs are brittle, unobservable, and unverifiable, making them unsuitable for production software engineering.
problem.zh: 核心问题是，尽管自动化代码生成取得了显著进展，但基于基础模型的自主软件工程代理在实际开发环境中仍然不可靠。先前工作中占主导地位的解释将这种不可靠性归因于模型能力的差距——即模型不够智能或缺乏足够的编码知识。本文认为这种解释是不完整且方向错误的。具体的差距在于，先前的工作几乎完全集中在改进模型本身（例如，更大的模型、更好的训练数据、微调），而忽略了中介模型与软件环境交互的运行时基础设施。在实践中，这一点很重要，因为即使是一个高度能力的模型，如果它不能正确观察项目的状态、从失败的构建或测试中接收有意义的反馈、将失败归因于特定的变更，或者验证修复是否完成，它也会失败。缺失的部分是一个系统化的、形式化的运行时基础——本文称之为“支架”——它结构化了代理的感知、行动、反馈和验证循环。没有这样的基础，代理的运行是脆弱的、不可观察且不可验证的，使其不适合生产级软件工程。

solution.en: The main contribution is the formalization of AI Harness Engineering as a distinct discipline and the proposal of a runtime substrate that sits between the foundation model and the software environment. The key insight is that autonomous software-engineering capability is not a property of the model alone, but of the composed model–harness–environment system. The harness mediates how the agent observes a project, acts on it, receives feedback, and establishes that a change is complete. This differs from prior work by shifting the locus of improvement from model capability to system design. The paper identifies eleven component responsibilities of the harness: task specification, context selection, tool access, project memory, task state, observability, failure attribution, verification, permissions, entropy auditing, and intervention recording. It operationalizes this through a four-level ladder (H0–H3) that progressively exposes runtime support to the agent, and a trace-based evaluation protocol that converts each agent run into an auditable episode package. The framework reframes the central question from 'can the model produce a patch?' to 'can the model–harness–environment system produce a verifiably correct, attributed, and maintainable change?'
solution.zh: 主要贡献是将AI支架工程形式化为一门独立的学科，并提出了一个位于基础模型和软件环境之间的运行时基础。关键见解是，自主软件工程能力不仅仅是模型本身的属性，而是组合的模型-支架-环境系统的属性。支架中介了代理如何观察项目、对其采取行动、接收反馈以及确定变更已完成。这与先前工作的不同之处在于，它将改进的重点从模型能力转移到系统设计。本文确定了支架的十一个组件职责：任务规范、上下文选择、工具访问、项目记忆、任务状态、可观察性、失败归因、验证、权限、熵审计和干预记录。它通过一个四级阶梯（H0–H3）来操作化这些职责，该阶梯逐步向代理暴露运行时支持，并采用基于轨迹的评估协议，将每次代理运行转换为一个可审计的回合包。该框架将核心问题从“模型能否生成补丁？”重新定义为“模型-支架-环境系统能否产生一个可验证正确、可归因且可维护的变更？”

methodology.en: The paper proposes a full technical framework for AI Harness Engineering. The system is composed of three parts: the foundation model (providing latent reasoning and coding capability), the software environment (providing repositories, tests, tools, logs, and build affordances), and the AI Harness Engineering substrate that sits between them. The harness mediates context, actions, feedback, and verification evidence. The paper identifies eleven component responsibilities: (1) task specification—defining the goal in a machine-readable form; (2) context selection—choosing which files, symbols, and dependencies to expose to the model; (3) tool access—providing a controlled set of actions (e.g., read file, write file, run test, git commit); (4) project memory—maintaining a persistent record of the agent's exploration and decisions; (5) task state—tracking progress toward completion; (6) observability—logging all model inputs and outputs for debugging; (7) failure attribution—linking test failures or build errors to specific code changes; (8) verification—running deterministic checks to confirm correctness; (9) permissions—controlling which actions the agent is allowed to take; (10) entropy auditing—measuring the unpredictability or randomness in agent behavior; and (11) intervention recording—logging any human overrides. These responsibilities are operationalized through a four-level ladder: H0 (no harness—the model directly produces a patch with no runtime support), H1 (basic harness—provides tool access and simple feedback loops), H2 (intermediate harness—adds project memory, task state tracking, and failure attribution), and H3 (full harness—adds deterministic verification, permissions, entropy auditing, and structured intervention recording). The paper also proposes a trace-based evaluation protocol that converts each agent run into an auditable episode package containing the full interaction log, all tool outputs, failure attributions, verification results, and a structured report. The method figure (Figure 1) depicts the model–harness–environment system: the foundation model sits at the top, the software environment at the bottom, and the AI Harness Engineering substrate in the middle, with arrows showing the flow of context (from harness to model), actions (from model to environment via harness), feedback (from environment to model via harness), and verification evidence (from harness to evaluator). The caption states that autonomous software-engineering capability is a property of the composed system, not of the model alone, and that when the harness is well-designed, the system performs as if the model is competent; when absent or unstable, the same model appears incompetent.
methodology.zh: 本文提出了一个完整的AI支架工程技术框架。该系统由三部分组成：基础模型（提供潜在推理和编码能力）、软件环境（提供仓库、测试、工具、日志和构建能力）以及位于它们之间的AI支架工程基础。支架中介了上下文、行动、反馈和验证证据。本文确定了十一个组件职责：(1) 任务规范——以机器可读的形式定义目标；(2) 上下文选择——选择向模型暴露哪些文件、符号和依赖项；(3) 工具访问——提供一组受控的动作（例如，读取文件、写入文件、运行测试、git提交）；(4) 项目记忆——维护代理探索和决策的持久记录；(5) 任务状态——跟踪完成进度；(6) 可观察性——记录所有模型输入和输出以进行调试；(7) 失败归因——将测试失败或构建错误链接到特定的代码变更；(8) 验证——运行确定性检查以确认正确性；(9) 权限——控制允许代理采取哪些动作；(10) 熵审计——衡量代理行为中的不可预测性或随机性；(11) 干预记录——记录任何人工覆盖。这些职责通过一个四级阶梯来操作化：H0（无支架——模型直接生成补丁，无运行时支持）、H1（基本支架——提供工具访问和简单的反馈循环）、H2（中级支架——添加项目记忆、任务状态跟踪和失败归因）以及H3（完整支架——添加确定性验证、权限、熵审计和结构化的干预记录）。本文还提出了一种基于轨迹的评估协议，将每次代理运行转换为一个可审计的回合包，包含完整的交互日志、所有工具输出、失败归因、验证结果和结构化报告。方法图（图1）描绘了模型-支架-环境系统：基础模型位于顶部，软件环境位于底部，AI支架工程基础位于中间，箭头显示了上下文（从支架到模型）、行动（从模型通过支架到环境）、反馈（从环境通过支架到模型）和验证证据（从支架到评估者）的流动。图注指出，自主软件工程能力是组合系统的属性，而非模型单独所有，并且当支架设计良好时，系统表现得好像模型是胜任的；当支架缺失或不稳定时，同一个模型看起来则不胜任。

experiments.en: The paper applies the framework to a controlled validation task. The setup involves a single foundation model (not named, but presumably a standard LLM) and a single software engineering task (not specified in detail, but described as a 'controlled validation task'). The experiment compares the four harness levels (H0, H1, H2, H3) on the same model and task. The evaluation uses the proposed trace-based protocol, producing episode packages for each run. The concrete results show that the evidence structure of the episode packages varies systematically with harness level: lower levels (H0) produce only a final patch with no supporting evidence; H1 adds basic tool output logs; H2 adds reproduction logs (showing how to reproduce the issue), failure attributions (linking test failures to specific code changes), and deterministic requirement checks; H3 adds structured verification reports (including permissions logs, entropy audit data, and intervention records). The paper does not report traditional metrics like pass@k or accuracy numbers; instead, it focuses on the qualitative and structural differences in the evidence produced. The key finding is that higher harness levels yield richer, more auditable, and more verifiable episode packages, demonstrating that the harness systematically affects the quality and trustworthiness of the agent's output. No baselines from prior work are compared, as the paper is primarily a framework proposal rather than an empirical benchmark.
experiments.zh: 本文将框架应用于一个受控的验证任务。设置涉及一个单一的基础模型（未命名，但推测是标准的大语言模型）和一个单一的软件工程任务（未详细说明，但描述为“受控验证任务”）。实验在相同的模型和任务上比较了四个支架级别（H0、H1、H2、H3）。评估使用提出的基于轨迹的协议，为每次运行生成回合包。具体结果显示，回合包的证据结构随支架级别系统性地变化：较低的级别（H0）仅产生一个最终的补丁，无支持证据；H1添加了基本的工具输出日志；H2添加了复现日志（显示如何复现问题）、失败归因（将测试失败链接到特定的代码变更）和确定性需求检查；H3添加了结构化的验证报告（包括权限日志、熵审计数据和干预记录）。本文没有报告像pass@k或准确率这样的传统指标；相反，它侧重于所产生证据的质量和结构差异。关键发现是，更高的支架级别产生更丰富、更可审计且更可验证的回合包，表明支架系统性地影响代理输出的质量和可信度。没有与先前工作的基线进行比较，因为本文主要是框架提案而非经验性基准。

conclusion.en: The paper finds that the harness level systematically determines the evidence structure of agent runs, with higher levels producing reproduction logs, failure attributions, and verification reports rather than just a final patch. This shifts the central research question from whether a model can produce a patch to whether the system can produce a verifiably correct, attributed, and maintainable change. The trace-based evaluation protocol addresses limitations of pass@k by providing auditable episode packages that capture execution, integration, and maintainability. However, the framework does not address integration with post-training techniques like RL or human-in-the-loop feedback, remaining a static runtime substrate rather than a training platform. For researchers, this means that future work should prioritize building and standardizing harness components to enable verifiable and maintainable autonomous software engineering, while complementary model-level improvements remain separate.
conclusion.zh: 本文发现，支架级别系统性地决定了代理运行的证据结构，更高的级别产生复现日志、失败归因和验证报告，而不仅仅是最终的补丁。这将核心研究问题从模型能否生成补丁转变为系统能否产生一个可验证正确、可归因且可维护的变更。基于轨迹的评估协议通过提供捕获执行、集成和可维护性的可审计回合包，解决了pass@k的局限性。然而，该框架没有解决与训练后技术（如强化学习或人机循环反馈）的集成问题，仍然是一个静态的运行时基础而非训练平台。对于研究人员来说，这意味着未来的工作应优先构建和标准化支架组件，以实现可验证和可维护的自主软件工程，而模型级别的改进则保持独立。

question.en: How does the AI Harness Engineering framework's trace-based evaluation protocol address the limitations of pass@k on isolated functions, and what specific evidence structures does it produce that could serve as more realistic benchmarks for repository-level code generation?
question.zh: AI支架工程框架的基于轨迹的评估协议如何解决pass@k在孤立函数上的局限性，并且它产生了哪些具体的证据结构，可以作为仓库级代码生成的更现实基准？

reason.en: This question directly targets the user's interest in evaluation methodologies beyond pass@k. The paper proposes a trace-based evaluation that produces episode packages with reproduction logs, failure attributions, and verification reports, which aligns with the user's desire for metrics that measure compilation, execution, and integration with existing codebases.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接针对用户对超越pass@k的评估方法的兴趣。本文提出了一种基于轨迹的评估，生成包含复现日志、失败归因和验证报告的回合包，这与用户希望衡量编译、执行以及与现有代码库集成的指标的需求一致。

question.en: In the context of the H0-H3 harness ladder, how could post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback be integrated into the harness to improve the agent's ability to produce verifiably correct patches at higher harness levels?
question.zh: 在H0-H3支架阶梯的背景下，像带有执行反馈的强化学习这样的训练后技术如何被集成到支架中，以提高代理在更高支架级别上生成可验证正确补丁的能力？

reason.en: This question connects the user's focus on post-training methods (RL with execution feedback) with the paper's core contribution of a runtime substrate. It probes how the harness could serve as a platform for incorporating execution feedback signals during training, potentially improving correctness on real-world software engineering tasks.
reason.zh: 这个问题将用户对训练后方法（带有执行反馈的强化学习）的关注与本文的核心贡献（运行时基础）联系起来。它探讨了支架如何作为平台，在训练期间整合执行反馈信号，从而可能提高在现实软件工程任务上的正确性。

question.en: What role does the 'context selection' component of the harness play in enabling long-context handling for repository-level tasks, and how does the framework's design address the challenge of integrating with existing codebases during generation?
question.zh: 支架的“上下文选择”组件在实现仓库级任务的长上下文处理中扮演什么角色，并且该框架的设计如何解决在生成过程中与现有代码库集成的挑战？

reason.en: This question targets the user's interest in long-context handling and repository-level generation. The paper identifies context selection as one of eleven harness responsibilities, and exploring how it manages project-level context directly addresses the user's question about how long-context models handle integration with existing codebases.
reason.zh: 这个问题针对用户对长上下文处理和仓库级生成的兴趣。本文将上下文识别为十一个支架职责之一，探索它如何管理项目级上下文直接解决了用户关于长上下文模型如何处理与现有代码库集成的问题。

question.en: How does the harness's 'failure attribution' component provide a mechanism for human-in-the-loop feedback, and could this be leveraged to create preference learning signals (e.g., for RLHF) that improve the model's ability to produce maintainable changes?
question.zh: 支架的“失败归因”组件如何提供人机循环反馈的机制，并且能否利用它来创建偏好学习信号（例如，用于基于人类反馈的强化学习），从而提高模型生成可维护变更的能力？

reason.en: This question addresses the user's interest in human-in-the-loop usage and preference learning. The paper's failure attribution and intervention recording components could generate structured feedback that aligns with RLHF/RLAIF approaches, offering a concrete pathway for incorporating human preferences into the code generation pipeline.
reason.zh: 这个问题涉及用户对人机循环使用和偏好学习的兴趣。本文的失败归因和干预记录组件可以生成结构化的反馈，与基于人类反馈的强化学习/基于AI反馈的强化学习方法相一致，为将人类偏好纳入代码生成流程提供了一条具体途径。

question.en: How does the AI Harness Engineering framework's trace-based evaluation protocol address the limitations of pass@k on isolated functions, and what specific evidence structures does it produce that could serve as more realistic benchmarks for repository-level code generation?
question.zh: AI支架工程框架的基于轨迹的评估协议如何解决pass@k在孤立函数上的局限性，并且它产生了哪些具体的证据结构，可以作为仓库级代码生成的更现实基准？

reason.en: This question directly targets the user's interest in evaluation methodologies beyond pass@k. The paper proposes a trace-based evaluation that produces episode packages with reproduction logs, failure attributions, and verification reports, which aligns with the user's desire for metrics that measure compilation, execution, and integration with existing codebases.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接针对用户对超越pass@k的评估方法的兴趣。本文提出了一种基于轨迹的评估，生成包含复现日志、失败归因和验证报告的回合包，这与用户希望衡量编译、执行以及与现有代码库集成的指标的需求一致。

answer.en: The AI Harness Engineering framework's trace-based evaluation protocol addresses the limitations of pass@k on isolated functions by shifting from a single-patch metric to a holistic, auditable record of the entire agent-environment interaction. The paper states that "lower levels produce only a final patch, higher levels produce reproduction logs, failure attributions, deterministic requirement checks, and structured verification reports." This directly counters pass@k's inability to capture whether a generated function compiles, executes correctly, or integrates with existing code. The specific evidence structures produced are "episode packages" that include "reproduction logs, failure attributions, and verification reports" (as noted in the abstract). These packages provide a multi-faceted record—covering the agent's actions, runtime feedback, and outcome verification—which can serve as more realistic benchmarks for repository-level code generation because they measure not just syntactic correctness but also execution, integration, and maintainability within a project context.
answer.zh: AI支架工程框架的基于轨迹的评估协议通过从单一补丁指标转向对整个代理-环境交互的全面、可审计记录，解决了pass@k在孤立函数上的局限性。论文指出，“较低的级别仅产生一个最终的补丁，而较高的级别则产生复现日志、失败归因、确定性需求检查和结构化的验证报告。”这直接反驳了pass@k无法捕获生成的函数是否编译、正确执行或与现有代码集成的问题。所产生的具体证据结构是“回合包”，其中包括“复现日志、失败归因和验证报告”（如摘要所述）。这些包提供了一个多方面的记录——涵盖代理的动作、运行时反馈和结果验证——可以作为仓库级代码生成的更现实基准，因为它们衡量的不仅是语法正确性，还包括项目上下文中的执行、集成和可维护性。

question.en: In the context of the H0-H3 harness ladder, how could post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback be integrated into the harness to improve the agent's ability to produce verifiably correct patches at higher harness levels?
question.zh: 在H0-H3支架阶梯的背景下，像带有执行反馈的强化学习这样的训练后技术如何被集成到支架中，以提高代理在更高支架级别上生成可验证正确补丁的能力？

reason.en: This question connects the user's focus on post-training methods (RL with execution feedback) with the paper's core contribution of a runtime substrate. It probes how the harness could serve as a platform for incorporating execution feedback signals during training, potentially improving correctness on real-world software engineering tasks.
reason.zh: 这个问题将用户对训练后方法（带有执行反馈的强化学习）的关注与本文的核心贡献（运行时基础）联系起来。它探讨了支架如何作为平台，在训练期间整合执行反馈信号，从而可能提高在现实软件工程任务上的正确性。

answer.en: The paper does not discuss integrating post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback into the harness ladder. It focuses on the harness as a runtime substrate that "mediates how a foundation-model agent observes a project, acts on it, receives feedback, and establishes that a change is complete" and proposes a four-level ladder (H0-H3) that "progressively exposes runtime support to the agent." The paper's trace-based evaluation protocol "converts each agent run into an auditable episode package" and shows that higher harness levels produce "reproduction logs, failure attributions, deterministic requirement checks, and structured verification reports." However, the paper does not address how RL training signals or execution feedback loops could be integrated into the harness to improve patch correctness at higher levels; it treats the harness as a static substrate for inference-time execution, not as a training platform.
answer.zh: 本文没有讨论将诸如带有执行反馈的强化学习之类的训练后技术集成到支架阶梯中。它专注于支架作为运行时基础，“中介了基础模型代理如何观察项目、对其采取行动、接收反馈并确定变更已完成”，并提出了一个四级阶梯（H0-H3），该阶梯“逐步向代理暴露运行时支持”。本文的基于轨迹的评估协议“将每次代理运行转换为一个可审计的回合包”，并表明更高的支架级别会产生“复现日志、失败归因、确定性需求检查和结构化的验证报告”。然而，本文没有涉及如何将强化学习训练信号或执行反馈循环集成到支架中以提高更高级别补丁的正确性；它将支架视为推理时执行的静态基础，而不是训练平台。

question.en: What role does the 'context selection' component of the harness play in enabling long-context handling for repository-level tasks, and how does the framework's design address the challenge of integrating with existing codebases during generation?
question.zh: 支架的“上下文选择”组件在实现仓库级任务的长上下文处理中扮演什么角色，并且该框架的设计如何解决在生成过程中与现有代码库集成的挑战？

reason.en: This question targets the user's interest in long-context handling and repository-level generation. The paper identifies context selection as one of eleven harness responsibilities, and exploring how it manages project-level context directly addresses the user's question about how long-context models handle integration with existing codebases.
reason.zh: 这个问题针对用户对长上下文处理和仓库级生成的兴趣。本文将上下文识别为十一个支架职责之一，探索它如何管理项目级上下文直接解决了用户关于长上下文模型如何处理与现有代码库集成的问题。

answer.en: The paper identifies 'context selection' as one of eleven harness responsibilities but does not elaborate on its specific role in enabling long-context handling for repository-level tasks. The abstract states the harness 'mediates how a foundation-model agent observes a project, acts on it, receives feedback, and establishes that a change is complete,' and lists 'context selection' among the component responsibilities. However, the paper provides no further detail on how context selection operates or how the framework's design addresses integration with existing codebases during generation. The discussion focuses on the harness's overall mediating role and the four-level ladder (H0-H3) for progressively exposing runtime support, but it does not specifically address long-context handling or codebase integration challenges. Therefore, the paper does not directly answer the question about context selection's role in long-context handling or integration with existing codebases.
answer.zh: 本文将“上下文选择”确定为十一个支架职责之一，但没有详细阐述其在实现仓库级任务长上下文处理中的具体作用。摘要指出，支架“中介了基础模型代理如何观察项目、对其采取行动、接收反馈并确定变更已完成”，并将“上下文选择”列为组件职责之一。然而，本文没有进一步提供关于上下文选择如何运作或该框架的设计如何解决生成过程中与现有代码库集成的细节。讨论集中在支架的整体中介作用和四级阶梯（H0-H3）以逐步暴露运行时支持，但没有具体涉及长上下文处理或代码库集成的挑战。因此，本文没有直接回答关于上下文选择在长上下文处理或与现有代码库集成中的作用的问题。

question.en: How does the harness's 'failure attribution' component provide a mechanism for human-in-the-loop feedback, and could this be leveraged to create preference learning signals (e.g., for RLHF) that improve the model's ability to produce maintainable changes?
question.zh: 支架的“失败归因”组件如何提供人机循环反馈的机制，并且能否利用它来创建偏好学习信号（例如，用于基于人类反馈的强化学习），从而提高模型生成可维护变更的能力？

reason.en: This question addresses the user's interest in human-in-the-loop usage and preference learning. The paper's failure attribution and intervention recording components could generate structured feedback that aligns with RLHF/RLAIF approaches, offering a concrete pathway for incorporating human preferences into the code generation pipeline.
reason.zh: 这个问题涉及用户对人机循环使用和偏好学习的兴趣。本文的失败归因和干预记录组件可以生成结构化的反馈，与基于人类反馈的强化学习/基于AI反馈的强化学习方法相一致，为将人类偏好纳入代码生成流程提供了一条具体途径。

answer.en: The paper does not describe a mechanism for human-in-the-loop feedback or preference learning signals within the 'failure attribution' component. The abstract lists 'failure attribution' as one of eleven component responsibilities and notes that higher harness levels produce 'failure attributions' as part of an 'auditable episode package,' but it provides no details on how a human could interact with that component to provide feedback or how such feedback could be leveraged for RLHF-style preference learning. The paper focuses on the harness as a runtime substrate that mediates agent-environment interaction and on trace-based evaluation, not on human-in-the-loop training signals. Therefore, the question is not addressed in the paper.
answer.zh: 本文没有描述“失败归因”组件中的人机循环反馈或偏好学习信号的机制。摘要将“失败归因”列为十一个组件职责之一，并指出更高的支架级别会产生“失败归因”作为“可审计回合包”的一部分，但没有提供关于人类如何与该组件交互以提供反馈或此类反馈如何用于基于人类反馈的强化学习风格的偏好学习的细节。本文专注于支架作为中介代理-环境交互的运行时基础以及基于轨迹的评估，而不是人机循环训练信号。因此，本文没有解决这个问题。
